title: Membrane permeability
description: Test your knowledge of membrane permeability!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best describes the selective permeability of the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cell membrane regulates the transport of specific substances into and out of the cell.
The cell membrane regulates the transport of all substances into and out of the cell.
The cell membrane regulates the transport of extracellular substances into, but not out of the cell.
The cell membrane regulates the transport of intracellular substances out of, but not into the cell.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements best explains how the cell membrane is selectively permeable?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The movement of specific substances into and out of the cell is controlled by the cell membrane.
The movement of all substances into and out of the cell is controlled by the cell membrane.
The movement of extracellular substances into, but not out of the cell is controlled by the cell membrane.
The movement of intracellular substances out of, but not into the cell is controlled by the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following most accurately describes the movement of substances across the lipid region of the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Gases such as $\text{N}_2$, $\text{O}_2$, and $\text{CO}_2$ can freely dissolve across the lipid region of the cell membrane.
Monosaccharides can freely dissolve across the lipid region of the cell membrane.
Small hydrophobic molecules require embedded transport proteins to dissolve across the lipid region of the cell membrane.
Uncharged molecules like $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ cannot dissolve across the lipid region of the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is true about the transportation of substances across the lipid region of the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Small nonpolar molecules can freely dissolve across the lipid region of the cell membrane.
Polysaccharides can freely dissolve across the lipid region of the cell membrane.
Gases such as $\text{N}_2$, $\text{O}_2$, and $\text{CO}_2$ require embedded transport proteins to dissolve across the lipid region of the cell membrane.
Ions can freely dissolve across the lipid region of the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains how substances are transported across the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Hydrophilic amino acids move across the cell membrane through embedded transport proteins.
Ions freely move across the cell membrane.
Nonpolar gases move across the cell membrane through embedded transport proteins.
Carbohydrates freely move across the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how substances move across the cell membrane?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Ions move across the cell membrane through embedded channel proteins.
Polar amino acids freely move across the cell membrane.
Hydrophilic amino acids freely move across the cell membrane.
Nucleic acids freely move across the cell membrane.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the permeability of the cell wall?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Some substances pass across the cell wall because it is a semipermeable structural boundary.
Substances cannot pass across the cell wall because it is an impermeable structural boundary.
All substances pass across the cell wall because it is a permeable structural boundary.
Only water passes across the cell wall because it is a semipermeable structural boundary.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the permeability of the cell wall?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cell wall is a semipermeable structural boundary that allows some substances to enter the internal environment.
The cell wall is an impermeable structural boundary that prevents all substances from entering the internal environment.
The cell wall is a permeable structural boundary that allows all substances to enter the internal environment.
The cell wall is a semipermeable structural boundary that only allows water to enter the internal environment.
